steps.title: Attach the relative clause
steps.content: The phrase after formation needs to modify the noun formation. The words some biologists believe can be used to explain a range of phenomena form a dependent relative clause, not a new independent sentence.

steps.title: Avoid false sentence breaks
steps.content: Choice A creates a fragment after the period. Choice B wrongly uses a semicolon before a dependent clause. Choice D incorrectly puts a comma before a restrictive relative clause. The remaining choice attaches the clause with that and no unnecessary punctuation. The answer is C.
